summary: Large ftaction decomp/type cleanup centered on fighter script CommandInfo parsing. The PR replaced many raw/placeholder command-union accesses with named bitfield structs in lb/types.h, rewrote several ftAction handlers to use NEXT_CMD/SKIP_CMD and semantic command fields, matched ftAction_80071B50 and ftAction_80071028, and greatly improved several other ftaction functions including ftAction_8007121C. It also renamed Fighter::x2214 to cmd_timer, corrected a few prototypes/signatures, and propagated the renamed/typed command fields into fighter and item command users.
what_changed: Reworked ftAction command handlers to read typed CommandInfo union members instead of anonymous placeholder fields such as test1/test2/unk22/unk7.

Added many CommandInfo bitfield structs in lb/types.h, including set_throw_flags, set_airborne_state, set_hitbox_damage, set_hitbox_scale, set_hitbox_x42_b57, set_cmd_var, set_hurt_state, set_jab_combo, set_jab_rapid, set_dobj_flags, set_throw_hitbox_0/1/2, set_article_vis, set_fighter_vis, set_tex_anim, spawn_gfx_0 through spawn_gfx_4, spawn_hitbox_0 through spawn_hitbox_5, sound_effect_0/1/2, pseudo_random_sfx, stage_sfx, footstep_fx, smash_charge, and wind_fx variants.

Matched ftAction_80071028 by using named spawn_gfx command chunks, NEXT_CMD advancement, and ftCo_8009F834 arguments for gfx_id, bone, common-bone selection, destroy-on-state-change, offset, range, and an extra float.

Substantially rewrote ftAction_8007121C from commented M2C/raw-field code into active C using named create_hitbox command chunks and HitCapsule fields for damage, size, offsets, angle, knockback, element, SFX, ground/aerial flags, and grabbed-victim behavior.
Changed several skip-style companion handlers from NEXT_CMD(cmd) to SKIP_CMD(cmd, 1), with a code remark that each CommandInfo handler appears to have a corresponding function capable of skipping it.
Renamed Fighter::x2214 to cmd_timer and updated its initialization and users in fighter.c, ftCo_ItemThrow.c, and ftaction.c.
Cleaned ftCo_800DEE84 signature from extra unused/int/char* parameters to Fighter_GObj*, u32, f32, f32 and named the damage multiplier parameter dmg_mult.
Removed an unused char* argument from ft_80084C38 in both implementation and header.
Adjusted item command code in it_2725.c to use set_throw_flags.hit_idx for item command variable assignments.

Added explanatory Doxygen-style @brief comments for several ftaction handlers such as spawning/skipping GFX, spawning/skipping hitboxes, adjusting hitbox damage/scale, setting hitbox flags, hurt capsule state, and jab combo.
smart_moves: Converted repeated raw bit extraction/M2C_FIELD command parsing into named bitfield structs, making later ftaction work searchable by command purpose rather than by offsets and unknown field names.
Kept command script parsing explicit with NEXT_CMD and SKIP_CMD, preserving the multi-word command format while making handler/skip-handler relationships easier to reason about.

Used semantic names where behavior was clear, such as cmd_timer, spawn_gfx_*, create_hitbox_*, set_airborne_state, set_hurt_state, set_jab_combo, set_throw_flags, set_hitbox_damage, and set_hitbox_scale.

Preserved uncertainty with @todo/@remarks comments instead of overclaiming, notably for possible reversed offset/range reads in ftAction_80071028 and the awkward matching cast to struct spawn_hitbox_skip in ftAction_8007121C.
Propagated type/signature fixes across headers and call sites, avoiding local-only matches that would leave stale prototypes in ft_081B.h and ft_0DF0.h.
Reduced placeholder noise in ftaction.c while retaining matching tactics such as PAD_STACK(8), temporary locals, and a casted skip struct where stack/layout pressure required it.
decomp_lessons: For fighter action-script decomp, first identify the command word boundaries and model each word as its own small bitfield struct; then advance with NEXT_CMD between words.

Skip companion functions should use SKIP_CMD for the same number of command words consumed by the real handler; this PR demonstrates SKIP_CMD(cmd, 5) for GFX/hitbox spawns and SKIP_CMD(cmd, 1) for single-word commands.
When script command data is packed in 1/256 fixed-point units, using (1 / 256.0f) can both document the scale and support matching in hitbox/GFX offset code.
Do not force semantic certainty too early: keep TODOs for suspected field-order issues like offset.x/z or range.x/z until verified in-game or against additional evidence.
Header cleanup can be part of matching work: removing unused prototype arguments or narrowing types to u32/f32 helps call sites and prevents misleading future decomp.

Large command-union refactors can temporarily make shared type headers messy; the author explicitly noted uncertainty about the cleanest lb/types.h structure, so future work should revisit organization/readability after matching pressure is reduced.
naming_conventions: Use command-purpose prefixes for CommandInfo union members: set_*, spawn_*, create_hitbox_*, sound_effect_*, pseudo_random_sfx_*, stage_sfx_*, footstep_fx_*, smash_charge_*, wind_fx_*.

Number multi-word script commands by command chunk suffixes, e.g. spawn_gfx_0 through spawn_gfx_4, spawn_hitbox_0 through spawn_hitbox_5, sound_effect_0 through sound_effect_2, set_throw_hitbox_0 through set_throw_hitbox_2.

Prefer semantic field names when known: id, hit_group, bone, use_common_bone_ids, damage, size, z_offset, y_offset, x_offset, angle, knockback_growth, weight_set_knockback, base_knockback, element, shield_damage, hit_sfx_severity, hit_sfx_kind, hit_aerial, hit_grounded.
Retain unknown names like unk*, x*_b* only where evidence is insufficient, as shown by remaining fields such as x1_b4, xF_b4, unkFloat, and x42_b57.
Rename Fighter fields when usage becomes clear across functions; x2214 became cmd_timer because it is set from cmd->timer and consumed by throw/item timing logic.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used NEXT_CMD(cmd) rather than manual pointer increments to match multi-word script command handlers while keeping source readable.
Used SKIP_CMD(cmd, n) for handlers that only advance over command payloads, replacing plain NEXT_CMD(cmd) in several skip stubs.
Used PAD_STACK(8) in ftAction_8007121C and an explicit cast to struct spawn_hitbox_skip* to preserve stack/codegen for a near-match.
Kept fixed-point conversions as (1 / 256.0f) multiplications for hitbox size/offsets and GFX offsets/ranges.
Split packed command data into narrow bitfield structs in lb/types.h to avoid repeated M2C_FIELD expressions while retaining the original packed layout.
Adjusted function signatures/prototypes to remove unused parameters and use f32/u32 where the matched code indicated those types.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the supplied slice.
Author note: the PR body says they made a mess in lb/types.h and were not sure what the cleanest way to make the structs was.
Bot report for GALE01 showed matched code improved by +0.02% / +776 bytes, with 2 new matches: ftAction_80071B50 reached 100.00% and ftAction_80071028 reached 100.00%.

Bot report also listed 7 unmatched-function improvements, including ftAction_8007121C to 99.51%, ftAction_80072320 to 97.44%, ftAction_80071FC8 to 96.93%, ftAction_80072E4C to 98.06%, ftAction_80072CD8 to 99.65%, ftAction_80073118 to 84.38%, and ftAction_80073008 to 68.59%.
follow_up_queries: Inspect later PRs touching lb/types.h CommandInfo to see whether the messy command struct organization from PR #1979 was cleaned up.
Search for subsequent renames of create_hitbox_* versus spawn_hitbox_* to determine the settled convention for hitbox command chunks.
Check whether the ftAction_80071028 offset/range TODO about reverse read order was later resolved or verified in-game.
Look for later work that completes ftAction_8007121C from 99.51% to 100% and whether it removes the spawn_hitbox_skip cast/PAD_STACK workaround.
Search for all cmd_timer users to confirm the x2214 rename remained stable.
